intro: Pteris matsuzaka, also known as Japanese brake fern or Matsuzaka brake fern, is a type of fern plant belonging to the genus Pteris. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Pteris matsuzaka include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Pteris matsuzaka is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, and is often used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
